message:
chore(beads): file 3 issues from real-world export-html testing

Three issues discovered during end-to-end testing of `cass export-html`
when exporting a Claude Code session for public sharing:

1. coding_agent_session_search-1hpvy [bug, p1]:
   export-html message count is misleading — reports "577 messages" for
   a 19-human-turn conversation. Root cause: lib.rs:12404 counts all
   filtered messages (tool results, assistant turns, system injections)
   instead of distinguishing human turns from tool machinery.

2. coding_agent_session_search-zy9l5 [bug, p1]:
   --no-cdns produces illegible code blocks (black on dark background).
   Root cause: the no-prism CSS fallback class is only added by CDN
   onerror handlers (template.rs:288), which never fire when CDN tags
   are omitted. The fallback CSS at template.rs:91-97 is dead code in
   the --no-cdns path.

3. coding_agent_session_search-2zmeg [feature, p2]:
   No session discovery command for agents. An agent trying to find its
   own session file took 6 attempts guessing path structures. Proposed:
   `cass sessions --current --json` using CWD-to-project-dir matching.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
